fields.description:
# Emit canonical signed plan-material pins

## Goal
Bind every newly certified plan review to the narrow plan material of its direct children and direct prerequisites, without adding a ticket field or user-facing command.

## Implementation
- Extend the deterministic manifest so pin lines appear after optional verified-at-sha and before dep lines: plan-material-pin: <role> <canonical-ticket-id> <material-fingerprint>. Roles are exactly child and prerequisite; sort lexicographically by role (child before prerequisite), then lexicographically by canonical ID. A target in both roles has two pins; duplicate edges within one role collapse. Each fingerprint is the existing 16-lowercase-hex material_fingerprint(PlanContext) value for the target ticket, never a file dependency hash. Define ManifestFormatError and manifest_pins() in manifest.py; attest.py imports and re-exports both beside the existing manifest helpers. manifest_pins() returns [] only when pin lines are absent and raises ManifestFormatError for any duplicate role+ID, unknown role, missing/extra token, invalid canonical ID, or non-16-hex fingerprint.
- Define PlanRelationSnapshot, PlanMaterialPin, PlanRelationSnapshotError, and tracker_head_sha() in plan_review/relation_snapshot.py, which imports no signing or orchestration modules. PlanRelationSnapshot contains subject_state, ticket_states_by_id, child_ids, prerequisite_ids, related_material, and ticket_store_revision. related_material is a deterministic tuple of {role, canonical_id, material_fingerprint} records sorted in manifest order and is the sole fingerprint carrier. Build exactly one store-wide ticket_states_by_id preload per review by reducing every ticket directory, including archived states; this explicit O(store) map is required to discover arbitrary inbound blockers. Collect outgoing depends_on from the subject and inbound blocks by scanning relation entries across that full map; do not call find_inbound_relationships because its active-source filter intentionally omits archived tickets. Resolve aliases and prefixes before deduplication; do not repeat store-wide reductions per target. Child 7c27 owns registration, schemas, prompts, and routing for the future prerequisite criterion; this story exposes PlanRelationSnapshot as its typed input boundary but does not implement or assume that criterion exists.
- Emit exactly one pin per canonical ID and role, in deterministic order. A ticket appearing in both supported roles receives one entry for each role; duplicate graph edges do not create duplicate entries.
- Run pin collection before prerequisite LLM evaluation. A target is readable only when its reference resolves uniquely to an existing reducer-readable state with matching canonical ID; archived and empty-description states qualify. Missing/deleted, ambiguous/malformed, reducer-error, canonical-ID-mismatch, or ticket-store-read failures raise PlanRelationSnapshotError whose reason is exactly missing-target, ambiguous-reference, malformed-reference, reducer-error, canonical-id-mismatch, or store-read-failure, with optional target reference. The review boundary catches it before any LLM call, emits an ERROR record exactly {event: plan_relation_snapshot_error, reason: <enum>, canonical_id: string|null, reference: string|null}, and returns unsigned INDETERMINATE remediation through CLI and MCP; the error never produces a partial pin set or traceback fallback.
- tracker_head_sha(tracker) follows snapshot_head rev-parse behavior but adds new cleanliness checks: after ensure_fresh, run git -C <tracker> status --porcelain and git -C <tracker> ls-files -u; any output raises PlanRelationSnapshotError(reason=store-read-failure, reference=<tracker>). Otherwise git -C <tracker> rev-parse HEAD must return one 40-lowercase-hex SHA, stored as ticket_store_revision. Missing git, invalid paths, nonzero subprocess exits, IO errors, and malformed output are all caught and re-raised as the same store-read-failure without exposing raw exceptions. This story verifies accurate token capture only; it never acquires the sign lock. Child 5967 exclusively owns before/after mismatch retries, under-lock HEAD re-verification, atomic append+commit, structured retry logging, and exhaustion behavior.
- Extend build_manifest(..., *, pins: Sequence[PlanMaterialPin] = ()) so absent pins preserve current manifest bytes exactly. Every plan-review signature-producing path—ordinary sign_plan_review, refresh_attestation, and resign_plan_review—calls collect_plan_relation_snapshot(ticket_id, repo_root) immediately before building its new manifest and passes related_material; refresh_attestation gains no caller-supplied relation state. If a referenced target was deleted after the prior PASS, refresh and re-sign return the same unsigned INDETERMINATE missing-target remediation until the stale relationship is repaired, rather than preserving stale certification. No new plan-review attestation can be minted pinless. Pin collection runs identically with enforcement enabled or disabled. Do not alter parent/child review prompts and do not add CLI/MCP operations. This story lands only on the epic feature branch: optional pins preserve old behavior until child 5967 atomically activates relation collection across all signing paths, and the feature is not deployed between those changes.
- Preserve new-reader parsing of legacy manifests with no pins. Add a pre-feature-verifier fixture proving it authenticates the stored full DSSE manifest including unknown pin lines, then ignores only those lines for field interpretation; it must not reconstruct or rehash a filtered manifest.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Passing reviews emit one deterministic pin for every direct child.
- [ ] Passing reviews emit one deterministic pin for every normalized prerequisite.
- [ ] Pins use authoritative canonical ticket IDs.
- [ ] Pin roles are exactly child or prerequisite.
- [ ] Pin fingerprints equal the target material_fingerprint(PlanContext) 16-hex value.
- [ ] Every stored reference is canonicalized before pin storage.
- [ ] Duplicate edges collapse within each role.
- [ ] Both prerequisite relation directions normalize to role prerequisite.
- [ ] Archived inbound-block sources remain discoverable.
- [ ] Empty-description targets remain readable.
- [ ] Every unreadable or unresolvable target returns unsigned INDETERMINATE before any LLM call.
- [ ] Every named PlanRelationSnapshotError reason produces the exact structured ERROR record without a traceback fallback.
- [ ] PlanRelationSnapshot related_material contains every normalized relation key.
- [ ] Each related_material record carries the exact target material fingerprint.
- [ ] tracker_head_sha returns the clean tickets-tracker 40-hex HEAD after ensure_fresh.
- [ ] Dirty, unmerged, subprocess, IO, path, or malformed-output failures map to store-read-failure.
- [ ] Enabled enforcement does not change emitted pin membership.
- [ ] Disabled enforcement does not change emitted pin membership.
- [ ] Ordinary signing emits the complete pin set.
- [ ] Progressive drift refresh emits the complete current pin set.
- [ ] Re-signing emits the complete current pin set.
- [ ] manifest_pins() returns [] only when pin lines are absent.
- [ ] Every malformed pin variant raises ManifestFormatError from manifest.py.
- [ ] A pre-feature reader authenticates the complete stored manifest, then ignores additive pin lines for field interpretation.

## Verification
- pytest -q tests/unit/test_plan_material_pins.py tests/unit/test_attest_opcert.py proves manifest encoding, all parser failures, legacy byte compatibility, attest.py re-exports, and unchanged public surfaces.
- pytest -q tests/unit/test_plan_relation_snapshot.py proves canonical collection, archived inbound sources, tracker HEAD capture, every exact abort reason, structured logs, and pre-LLM termination.
- pytest -q tests/unit/test_plan_review_resign.py proves ordinary sign, progressive drift refresh, and re-sign emit complete pins with enforcement both enabled and disabled.
- Child 5967 separately runs the production-path revision-mismatch and atomic-signing integration tests; those behaviors are outside this story.
- Run make check and the non-integration test suite.
